steps.title: Add 9 to both sides to isolate the product
steps.content:
The equation $(d-28)(d+28)-9=-9$ has $-9$ on both sides. Adding $9$ to both sides eliminates these constants:
$$(d-28)(d+28) = 0$$

steps.title: Apply the zero product property
steps.content:
A product equals zero if and only if at least one factor equals zero. Therefore:
$$d - 28 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad d + 28 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve each factor for d
steps.content:
From the first equation: $d = 28$
From the second equation: $d = -28$

Both values satisfy the original equation. One solution is $d = 28$ — 28 is correct.
